rationale: The pair appears potentially substantive because both papers examine M&A value creation through marketing/R&D-related firm resources and abnormal stock returns (citing: strategic emphasis alignment in marketing vs. R&D and announcement CARs; cited: product capital, selection/deployment, and long-term abnormal returns). The citing note explicitly situates itself as extending resource-based merger research, and the cited paper is a close prior in that same acquisition-performance stream rather than an incidental background mention.
